MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DANIEL BALSAM, TEL. NO. 869-2873, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: FORMER DEFENSE COUNSEL BENNET KELLEY'S Motion For Relief from Order Granting Monetary Sanctions Pursuant to CCP 473 (B), And For A Finding Of Contempt Of Court IS Denied without prejudice of refiling motion of good showing. While the title of the motion purports to seek issue sanctions, plaintiff's papers do not state what issue sanctions are sought. The portion of the motion seeking monetary sanctions for total non-responsiveness to the prior order's requirement that further discovery responses be provided is, in legal effect, a request for a finding of contempt and imposition of contempt sanctions. However, no finding of contempt, much less imposition of any contempt sanctions, can be made without following the specified procedure for contempt, including the issuance of an order to show re contempt, which plaintiff has neither done nor requested. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported =302/HEK

APPEARANCES: Daniel L. Balsam, Esq. of The Law Offices of Daniel Balsam 869-2873 pltf & moving party; no other appearances.
